evidence.summary: Project has 843 total commits across all branches, spanning from 2026-03-31 to 2026-06-02 (approx. 2 months). April 2026 had 408 commits and May 2026 had 430 commits, indicating extremely high development velocity. The project originated from a leaked source code archive (src.zip) and was made locally runnable.

Primary contributor is '程序员阿江(Relakkes)' with 732 commits (~87% of total), followed by 'Relakkes Yang' (25), '程序员阿江-Relakkes' (25), 'pobb' (19), 'Robin11' (6), 'cynicalight' (6), 'Robin' (5). Multiple contributor name variants for the same primary author suggest identity/account changes. Community contributions are minimal but present (sigridjineth, 言志伟, chenziyang, etc.).

Branch strategy: main branch is the primary development branch. Current branch is '008-specify-discussions-settings'. Other local branches include '007-workflow-phase-skills'. Remote branches include 'origin/006-agent-workflow-authoring-tools' and 'origin/main'. The numbered branch naming (006, 007, 008) suggests a sequential feature-workflow approach.

Project evolution phases: (1) Init phase (2026-03-31): source code import from src.zip, README creation, bilingual docs; (2) Bootstrap phase (Apr 2026): Azure OpenAI support, /buddy command, third-party model integration, FAQ, issue templates; (3) Feature expansion (Apr-May 2026): desktop app (Tauri), goal system, agent teams, plugin system, Feishu IM integration, workflow session mode; (4) Spec Kit Plus phase (May-Jun 2026): .specify/ scaffolding, project cognition, skill system, workflow authoring tools.

Active development areas from commit messages: (1) Desktop/Tauri app - most frequently changed area with UI improvements, tab management, proxy support, zoom, file drag-drop; (2) Goal system - goal commands, goal sessions, background work, goal UI; (3) Agent system - team member lifecycle, agent progress display, subagent readability; (4) Plugin/skill system - slash commands, plugin skills, workflow session mode; (5) .specify/ - Spec Kit Plus scaffolding, project cognition, skill definitions.

External community contributions came via pull requests: PR #447 from 1506086927 (portable mode), PR #3 from NanmiCoder (teams lifecycle), PR #1 from 1506086927 (teams state persistence), PR #27 from studyzy (docs), PR #2 from dhb52 (docs). The project also synced with upstream (cc-haha repository) indicating it is a fork.

Commit message style: Mix of English conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:) and Chinese descriptive messages. Later commits (2026-05+) increasingly follow conventional commit format with type prefixes. The most recent commits focus on .specify/ skill system and workflow authoring.

attrs.description: Project timeline shows evolution from source import to desktop app to Spec Kit Plus scaffolding, with development focus shifting accordingly
Primary contributor drove 87% of the project's 843 commits over 2 months

edges.attrs: Numbered feature branches (006, 007, 008) organize the project's evolution phases

observations.summary: Rapid fork-to-product evolution: Project started as a source code import from a leaked archive on 2026-03-31 and within 2 months accumulated 843 commits, transforming into an actively developed desktop AI assistant application with Tauri-based desktop shell, goal system, agent teams, plugin architecture, and Spec Kit Plus integration.

Single-primary-author project: 程序员阿江(Relakkes) authored ~87% of all commits. This is a solo-maintainer project with occasional community PRs. The contributor uses multiple name variants (Relakkes Yang, 程序员阿江-Relakkes), likely across different machines or accounts. This concentration creates both velocity and bus-factor risk.

Sequential numbered feature branches (006, 007, 008) indicate a disciplined feature-workflow approach where each major initiative gets its own branch. Current branch '008-specify-discussions-settings' focuses on Spec Kit Plus discussion and settings features. This pattern suggests the project uses a lightweight variant of trunk-based development with numbered feature lanes.

Four distinct development phases visible in commit history: (1) Source import and documentation (late Mar 2026), (2) Core feature development - model support, commands, docs (early Apr 2026), (3) Desktop app and advanced features - Tauri shell, goal system, agent teams, plugins (Apr-May 2026), (4) Spec Kit Plus integration - .specify/ scaffolding, project cognition, skill system, workflow authoring (late May-Jun 2026). Each phase built upon the previous, showing organic architectural growth.

Upstream sync pattern: The project is a fork that periodically syncs with upstream (cc-haha repository). Merge commits like 'Merge upstream/main: 同步上游最新代码' and 'Merge branch main of https://github.com/chenziyang110/cc-haha' show active upstream tracking. This means the project inherits upstream improvements while adding local customizations.

Development velocity is exceptionally high: 838 commits in April-May 2026 combined (avg ~14 commits/day). This suggests rapid prototyping and iterative development. The commit message quality evolved from vague Chinese descriptions to more structured conventional commits over time, indicating growing development discipline.
